name: Kadane's Algorithm
summary: Walk the array once, tracking the best sum of a subarray ending at the current element: either extend the previous run or restart at this element. The running best of those is the answer.
visualizationIntent: init: array of values shown; max_sum and current_sum both start at nums[0]. loop: pointer i advances to the current element (current cell), earlier cells are visited. update (L6): the Kadane decision — current_sum becomes max(nums[i], current_sum + nums[i]); a readout shows 'restart nums[i] vs extend prev+nums[i]' and which wins. check (L7): if current_sum beats max_sum, the global best rises (key event). return: result panel holds the maximum subarray sum.

lineExplanations.3: Initialise the overall best sum to the first element.
lineExplanations.4: Track the best subarray sum ending at the current position, also seeded with the first element.
lineExplanations.5: Scan from the second element to the end.
lineExplanations.6: The Kadane step: either restart the run at nums[i] or extend the previous run — whichever is larger.
lineExplanations.7: Update the overall best if the current run beats it.
lineExplanations.8: Return the largest subarray sum found.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: — the class wrapper the platform expects your method to live inside.
syntaxExplanations.2: def maxSubArray(self, nums): — defines the method; nums is the integer array.
syntaxExplanations.3: max_sum = nums[0] — initialise the overall best to the first element.
syntaxExplanations.4: current_sum = nums[0] — the best subarray sum ending at the current position, seeded with the first element.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(1, len(nums)): — scan from the second element to the end.
syntaxExplanations.6: current_sum = max(nums[i], current_sum + nums[i]) — restart at nums[i] or extend the run, whichever is larger.
syntaxExplanations.7: max_sum = max(max_sum, current_sum) — keep the best run seen so far.
syntaxExplanations.8: return max_sum — the maximum subarray sum.
